  3. Fruit consists of a wall or pericarp and seeds. In coconut fruit is barry, the mesocarp is fibrous.
  4. In mango and coconut, the fruit is known as a drupe.
  5. In mango, the pericarp is well differentiated into an outer thin epicarp, a middle fleshy edible mesocarp, and an inner stony hard endocarp.
